description: The ultimate goal of Buddhist practice, the state of liberation from suffering, desire, and the cycle of rebirth. Literally means "extinguishing" - the extinction of greed, hatred, and delusion.
name: Buddhist Mythology

notions.negative: End of suffering, end of rebirth

mahayana.bodhisattva: Delay final nirvana to help others
mahayana.sunyata: Emptiness - nirvana and samsara not different

types.nirupadhishesa: Without remainder - at death of enlightened one

keyMyths.title: The Enlightenment of the Buddha
keyMyths.description: Siddhartha Gautama, after years of asceticism, attained enlightenment while meditating under the Bodhi tree. He overcame temptations and distractions to achieve Nirvana, becoming the Buddha.

keyMyths.title: The First Sermon at Sarnath
keyMyths.description: After his enlightenment, the Buddha delivered his first sermon in Sarnath, India, to five ascetics. In this sermon, he outlined the Four Noble Truths and the Eightfold Path, the foundations of Buddhist teachings.

keyMyths.title: Mara's Temptation
keyMyths.description: Mara, the personification of temptation and death, attempted to prevent Siddhartha from achieving enlightenment. He sent his daughters to seduce him and armies of demons to frighten him, but Siddhartha remained steadfast.

keyMyths.title: The Jataka Tales
keyMyths.description: The Jataka tales are a collection of stories about the previous lives of the Buddha, both in human and animal forms. These stories illustrate the virtues and qualities that the Buddha cultivated over countless lifetimes.

keyMyths.title: The Parinirvana of the Buddha
keyMyths.description: At the age of 80, the Buddha attained Parinirvana, the final passing away, releasing him from the cycle of rebirth. His last words encouraged his disciples to strive diligently for their own liberation.

sources.name: The Pali Canon
sources.description: The standard collection of scriptures in the Theravada Buddhist tradition, written in the Pali language.
sources.title: The Pali Canon

sources.name: Mahayana Sutras
sources.description: A vast collection of sutras accepted as scripture by Mahayana Buddhists.
sources.title: Mahayana Sutras

sources.name: Buddhacarita by Asvaghosa
sources.description: An epic poem on the life of the Buddha, written in Sanskrit.
sources.title: Buddhacarita by Asvaghosa

sources.name: Early Buddhist Texts
sources.description: Collection of scholarly articles by various authors concerning the earliest forms of Buddhist texts.
sources.title: Early Buddhist Texts

sources.name: Buddhist Hybrid Sanskrit Grammar and Dictionary
sources.description: Reference material regarding the language and lexicon of Buddhist texts written in Sanskrit.
sources.title: Buddhist Hybrid Sanskrit Grammar and Dictionary
